org.bytedeco.tensorrt.nvinfer

Class IAttention

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.bytedeco.javacpp.Pointer
      • org.bytedeco.tensorrt.nvinfer.INoCopy
        • org.bytedeco.tensorrt.nvinfer.IAttention
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    AutoCloseable

    @Namespace(value="nvinfer1")
 @NoOffset
 @Properties(inherit=nvinfer.class)
public class IAttention
extends INoCopy
    \class IAttention \brief Helper for constructing an attention that consumes query, key and value tensors. An attention subgraph implicitly includes three main components, two MatrixMultiply layers known as BMM1 and BMM2, and one normalization operation which defaults to be a Softmax. By default, IAttention is not decomposable and TensorRT will try to use a single fused kernel, which may be more efficient than if the subgraph is expressed without IAttention. Setting the IAttention to decomposable=True can allow IAttention to be decomposed to use multiple kernels if no fused kernel support found. Query Key Value Mask (optional) NormalizationQuantizeScale (optional) | | | | | | Transpose | | | | | | | | ----BMM1---- | | | | | | | *--------------------------- | | | | Normalization | | | | | *------------------------------------------------ | | -------BMM2------ | Output The attention has the following inputs, in order of input index: * Query contains the input query. It is a tensor of type kFLOAT, kHALF or kBF16 with shape [batchSize, numHeadsQuery, sequenceLengthQuery, dimHead] * Key contains the input key. It is a tensor of type kFLOAT, kHALF or kBF16 with shape [batchSize, numHeadsKeyValue, sequenceLengthKeyValue, dimHead] * Value contains the input value. It is a tensor of type kFLOAT, kHALF or kBF16 with shape [batchSize, numHeadsKeyValue, sequenceLengthKeyValue, dimHead] * Mask (optional) contains the mask value. It is a tensor of type kBOOL or the same data type of BMM1 output with shape [batchSize, numHeadsQuery, sequenceLengthQuery, sequenceLengthKeyValue] with batchSize and numHeadsQuery broadcastable. For a kBOOL mask, a True value indicates that the corresponding position is allowed to attend. For other data types, the mask values will be added to the BMM1 output, known as an add mask. * NormalizationQuantizeScale (optional) contains the quantization scale for the attention normalization output. It is a tensor of type kFLOAT, kHALF or kBF16 with dimension 0 or 1.
    See Also:
    for the complete matrix of fused kernel support. \warning Do not inherit from this class, as doing so will break forward-compatibility of the API and ABI.

      Modifier and Type Method and Description

      \brief Get whether the attention will run a causal inference.

      \brief Get whether the attention can be decomposed to use multiple kernels if no fused kernel support found.

      \brief Get the IAttention input corresponding to the given index.

      \brief Return the name of the attention.

      \brief Get the number of inputs of IAttention.

      \brief Get the number of outputs of a layer.

      \brief Get the normalization operation for the attention.

      \brief Get the quantization scale for the attention normalization output.

      \brief Get the datatype the attention normalization is quantized to.

      \brief Get the IAttention output corresponding to the given index.

      \brief Set whether the attention will run a causal inference.

      \brief Set whether the attention can be decomposed to use multiple kernels if no fused kernel support found.

      \brief Append or replace an input of this layer with a specific tensor.

      \brief Set whether a mask will be used for the normalization operation.

    • Constructor Detail

      • IAttention

        public IAttention(Pointer p)
        Pointer cast constructor. Invokes Pointer(Pointer).

    • Method Detail

      • setNormalizationOperation

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setNormalizationOperation(nvinfer.AttentionNormalizationOp op)
        \brief Set the normalization operation for the attention.
        Returns:
        True if the normalization operation is set successfully, false otherwise.
        See Also:
        getNormalizationOperation(), AttentionNormalizationOp

      • setNormalizationOperation

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setNormalizationOperation(@Cast(value="nvinfer1::AttentionNormalizationOp")
                                                                                       int op)

      • getNormalizationOperation

        @NoException(value=true)
public nvinfer.AttentionNormalizationOp getNormalizationOperation()
        \brief Get the normalization operation for the attention.
        Returns:
        The normalization operation for the attention. Default is kSOFTMAX.
        See Also:
        setNormalizationOperation(), AttentionNormalizationOp

      • setMask

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setMask(@ByRef
                                                                     ITensor mask)
        \brief Set whether a mask will be used for the normalization operation.
        Parameters:
        mask - the mask tensor of type kBOOL or the same data type of BMM1 output with 4d shape broadcastable to [batchSize, numHeadsQuery, sequenceLengthQuery, sequenceLengthKeyValue]. For a kBOOL mask, a True value indicates that the corresponding position is allowed to attend. For other data types, the mask values will be added to the BMM1 output, known as an add mask.
        Returns:
        True if the mask is set successfully, false otherwise.
        See Also:
        getMask

      • getMask

        @NoException(value=true)
public ITensor getMask()
        \brief Get the optional mask in attention.
        Returns:
        The optional mask in attention, nullptr if no mask is set.
        See Also:
        setMask

      • setCausal

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setCausal(@Cast(value="bool")
                                                                       boolean isCausal)
        \brief Set whether the attention will run a causal inference. Cannot be used together with setMask().
        Returns:
        True if the causal inference is set successfully, false otherwise.
        See Also:
        getCausal

      • getCausal

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean getCausal()
        \brief Get whether the attention will run a causal inference.
        Returns:
        True if the attention will run a causal inference, false otherwise. Default is false.
        See Also:
        setCausal

      • setDecomposable

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setDecomposable(@Cast(value="bool")
                                                                             boolean decomposable)
        \brief Set whether the attention can be decomposed to use multiple kernels if no fused kernel support found.
        Returns:
        True if the decomposable attention is set successfully, false otherwise.
        See Also:
        getDecomposable

      • getDecomposable

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean getDecomposable()
        \brief Get whether the attention can be decomposed to use multiple kernels if no fused kernel support found.
        Returns:
        True if the attention can be decomposed to use multiple kernels by the compiler, false otherwise. Default is false.
        See Also:
        setDecomposable

      • setInput

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setInput(int index,
                                                                      @ByRef
                                                                      ITensor input)
        \brief Append or replace an input of this layer with a specific tensor.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the input to modify.
        input - the new input tensor. The indices are as follows: Input 0 is the input query tensor. Input 1 is the input key tensor. Input 2 is the input value tensor.
        Returns:
        True if the input tensor is set successfully, false otherwise.

      • getNbInputs

        @NoException(value=true)
public int getNbInputs()
        \brief Get the number of inputs of IAttention. IAttention has three inputs.
        Returns:
        The number of inputs of IAttention.

      • getInput

        @NoException(value=true)
public ITensor getInput(int index)
        \brief Get the IAttention input corresponding to the given index.
        Parameters:
        index - The index of the input tensor.
        Returns:
        The input tensor, or nullptr if the index is out of range.

      • getNbOutputs

        @NoException(value=true)
public int getNbOutputs()
        \brief Get the number of outputs of a layer. IAttention has one output.

      • getOutput

        @NoException(value=true)
public ITensor getOutput(int index)
        \brief Get the IAttention output corresponding to the given index. IAttention has only one output.
        Parameters:
        index - The index of the output tensor.
        Returns:
        The indexed output tensor, or nullptr if the index is out of range.

      • setName

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setName(String name)
        \brief Set the name of the attention. The name is used in error diagnostics. This method copies the name string. \warning The string name must be null-terminated, and be at most 4096 bytes including the terminator.
        Returns:
        True if the name is set successfully, false otherwise.
        See Also:
        getName()

      • setName

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setName(@Cast(value="const char*")
                                                                     BytePointer name)

      • getName

        @NoException(value=true)
public String getName()
        \brief Return the name of the attention.
        Returns:
        The name of the attention.
        See Also:
        setName()

      • setNormalizationQuantizeScale

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setNormalizationQuantizeScale(@ByRef
                                                                                           ITensor tensor)
        \brief Set the quantization scale for the attention normalization output.
        Parameters:
        tensor - for quantization scale. Data type must be DataType::kFLOAT, DataType::kHALF or DataType::kBF16. Must be a 0-d or 1-d.
        Returns:
        True if the quantization scale is set successfully, false otherwise. \warning Must be used together with setNormalizationQuantizeToType to set normalization output datatype to DataType::kFP8 or DataType::kINT8.

      • getNormalizationQuantizeScale

        @NoException(value=true)
public ITensor getNormalizationQuantizeScale()
        \brief Get the quantization scale for the attention normalization output.
        Returns:
        The quantization scale for the attention normalization output or nullptr if no quantization scale is set.

      • setNormalizationQuantizeToType

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setNormalizationQuantizeToType(nvinfer.DataType type)
        \brief Set the datatype the attention normalization is quantized to.
        Parameters:
        type - the datatype the attention normalization is quantized to. Must be one of DataType::kFP8, DataType::kINT8.
        Returns:
        True if the quantization to type is set successfully, false otherwise.

      • setNormalizationQuantizeToType

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setNormalizationQuantizeToType(@Cast(value="nvinfer1::DataType")
                                                                                            int type)

      • getNormalizationQuantizeToType

        @NoException(value=true)
public nvinfer.DataType getNormalizationQuantizeToType()
        \brief Get the datatype the attention normalization is quantized to.
        Returns:
        The datatype the attention normalization is quantized to. The default value is DataType::kFLOAT. \warning Must be used after normalization quantization to type is set by setNormalizationQuantizeToType.

      • setMetadata

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setMetadata(String metadata)
        \brief Set the metadata for IAttention. The metadata is emitted in the JSON returned by IEngineInspector with ProfilingVerbosity set to kDETAILED.
        Parameters:
        metadata - The per-layer metadata. \warning The string name must be null-terminated and be at most 4096 bytes including the terminator.
        Returns:
        True if the metadata is set successfully, false otherwise.
        See Also:
        getMetadata(), getLayerInformation()

      • setMetadata

        @Cast(value="bool")
 @NoException(value=true)
public boolean setMetadata(@Cast(value="const char*")
                                                                         BytePointer metadata)

      • getMetadata

        @NoException(value=true)
public String getMetadata()
        \brief Get the metadata of IAttention.
        Returns:
        The metadata as a null-terminated C-style string. If setMetadata() has not been called, an empty string "" will be returned as a default value.
        See Also:
        setMetadata()
